perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) A young man wearing a white sweatshirt with a blue graphic and grey pants is seen squatting in a snowboarding-like pose in an airport terminal. (2) He maintains this exact pose across a series of rapid cuts in different environments: a bus stop sidewalk, in front of a Starbucks, on top of a sofa at home while wearing a ski helmet, on a residential street, in a game arcade, and inside a clothing store trying on various jackets. (3) The scene shifts to a real snowy mountain where the man, now in full orange and black snowboarding gear, is lying face-down in the snow with a snowboard attached to his feet. (4) He lifts his head to look at the camera with a tired but smiling expression.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) Upbeat, energetic, and rhythmic background music with a strong beat plays as the scenes cut rapidly. No vocals are present. (2) At the 12-second mark, as the scene shifts to the snow, the music transitions to a slower, more melodic acoustic style. (3) Soft male vocals begin singing "Nobody ever knows... nobody ever sees..." (4) Subdued ambient wind noise and the sound of snow rustling are audible in the final scene.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) Decorative Text: "POV:當你以為滑雪很簡單" (POV: When you think snowboarding is easy) appears as a fixed orange banner at the top throughout the entire video. The tone is humorous and ironic. (2) Functional Text: Japanese signage and store titles appear briefly in the background of various scenes.

temporal_pacing_summary.mainActionFlow: Expectation Montage -> Reality Transition -> Humorous Result

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 main male creator, approximately 3 passers-by in the background of the airport and street scenes.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: A young Asian male with dyed light brown hair. Initially wears a white sweatshirt and grey pants, later wears various ski jackets and eventually a full snowboarding suit with goggles.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: Diverse settings including an airport waiting area with grey chairs, a sidewalk near a Starbucks, a living room with a striped sofa, a game arcade with a medal machine, a clothing store with racks of winter coats, and a snowy ski slope with a lift in the background.
visual_scene_and_style.lightingStyle: Mixed: Bright indoor fluorescent, natural daylight, and overcast outdoor light on the mountain.

audio_music_and_environment.bgmGenre: Electronic Beat followed by Acoustic Pop

narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: To create humorous content by contrasting the 'practice' of a snowboarding pose in inappropriate daily life settings with the actual difficult and tiring reality of snowboarding on a mountain. It mocks the 'POV' trend where people make things look easy, only to show the exhausted and messy reality of a beginner.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: While brands like Burton and Starbucks are visible, they appear as organic background elements rather than paid placements.

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: Main creator consistently uses a fixed 'squat' pose throughout various cuts to maintain visual continuity.
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The video relies heavily on the 'Expectation vs. Reality' trope, which is highly effective for engagement in short-form video platforms.
